critical-assumption.title: Critical assumption
critical-assumption.prompt: Which critical, fragile assumption is this scorecard evaluating?
examples: Target customers will pay a monthly fee for automated reports
Channel partners will recommend us to existing customers

current-evidence.title: Current evidence
current-evidence.prompt: What real evidence exists now? Write test results, behavior data, or customer quotes — not opinions.
examples: 6 of 8 interviewees described an active workaround

evidence-strength.title: Evidence strength
evidence-strength.prompt: Is the evidence weak, medium, or strong? Why is it enough or not enough for the next step?
examples: Medium: clicks and signups but no payment

learning-velocity.title: Learning velocity
learning-velocity.prompt: How often does the team get decision-changing evidence? What was learned recently?
examples: We learned the budget owner is not the daily user

risk-reduction.title: Risk reduction
risk-reduction.prompt: How much have desirability, feasibility, and viability risks decreased? What remains untested?
examples: Technical feasibility tested; channel cost unknown

portfolio-decision.title: Portfolio decision
portfolio-decision.prompt: Based on evidence, should the team persevere, pivot, test more, transfer, fund, or retire?
examples: Pivot: test mid-market instead of SMB
Retire: pain is not strong enough

next-experiment.title: Next experiment
next-experiment.prompt: What is the cheapest next experiment that can change the decision? What are pass/fail criteria?
examples: Pre-sell to 5 target customers in 2 weeks; proceed only if ≥2 pay
Deliver 3 Wizard-of-Oz runs and test service cost
